news.title:
The Ramco Cements Ltd confirmed timely interest payment of Rs.15.6 Cr on NCDs due 12th March 2026.
No redemption payments were applicable or made for the Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs).
news.summary:
The Ramco Cements Ltd confirmed timely interest payment of Rs.15.6 Cr on NCDs due 12th March 2026.
No redemption payments were applicable or made for the Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs).

news.title:
Cement stocks fell up to 7% due to margin concerns from rising crude oil prices.
Ramco dropped 7% to ₹1,014.6; JK 6% to ₹5,100; UltraTech 5% to ₹11,350.4; India Cements 5% to ₹362.9.
news.summary:
Cement stocks fell up to 7% due to margin concerns from rising crude oil prices.
Ramco dropped 7% to ₹1,014.6; JK 6% to ₹5,100; UltraTech 5% to ₹11,350.4; India Cements 5% to ₹362.9.

news.title:
The Ramco Cements sold non-core assets worth Rs. 59.6 Cr to Rainbow Foundations.
Total disposals now Rs. 1080.8 Cr, exceeding Rs. 1000 Cr target; funds to reduce debt.
news.summary:
The Ramco Cements sold non-core assets worth Rs. 59.6 Cr to Rainbow Foundations.
Total disposals now Rs. 1080.8 Cr, exceeding Rs. 1000 Cr target; funds to reduce debt.

news.title:
Shree Cement, Dalmia Bharat, and Ramco Cements posted mixed Q3 FY26 results with notable capacity expansion plans projected at 7.4% CAGR till FY28.
Ramco Cements, Star Cement, and Shree Cement showed varied revenue growth, while Orient Cement reported a decline in both QoQ and YoY revenue.
news.summary:
Shree Cement, Dalmia Bharat, and Ramco Cements posted mixed Q3 FY26 results with notable capacity expansion plans projected at 7.4% CAGR till FY28.
Ramco Cements, Star Cement, and Shree Cement showed varied revenue growth, while Orient Cement reported a decline in both QoQ and YoY revenue.

news.title:
BOB Capital Markets issues a Sell report on The Ramco Cements.
The current market price of The Ramco Cements is Rs 1150.1.
news.summary:
BOB Capital Markets issues a Sell report on The Ramco Cements.
The current market price of The Ramco Cements is Rs 1150.1.

news.title:
Citi downgrades Ramco Cements to Sell due to high valuation.
Earnings risks persist despite observed volume growth, says Citi.
news.summary:
Citi downgrades Ramco Cements to Sell due to high valuation.
Earnings risks persist despite observed volume growth, says Citi.

news.title:
CLSA and Kotak Institutional Equities are bearish on Ramco Cements.
Investec downgrades Star Cement to sell, signaling caution.
news.summary:
CLSA and Kotak Institutional Equities are bearish on Ramco Cements.
Investec downgrades Star Cement to sell, signaling caution.

news.title: CLSA downgrades Ramco Cements to Underperform with TP cut to Rs 890.
news.summary: CLSA downgrades Ramco Cements to Underperform with TP cut to Rs 890.

news.title: Jefferies retains 'Hold' on Ramco Cement, raises target to Rs 1,045.
news.summary: Jefferies retains 'Hold' on Ramco Cement, raises target to Rs 1,045.

news.title: Ramco Cements shares decline due to weaker Q3 results and low cement prices.
news.summary: Ramco Cements shares decline due to weaker Q3 results and low cement prices.

news.title:
The Ramco Cements boosted Ariyalur grinding capacity to 5.5 MTPA.
Kolimigundla expansion raised cement capacity to 3 MTPA; FY27 targets 31.14 MTPA.
news.summary:
The Ramco Cements boosted Ariyalur grinding capacity to 5.5 MTPA.
Kolimigundla expansion raised cement capacity to 3 MTPA; FY27 targets 31.14 MTPA.

news.title:
The Ramco Cements' Q3FY26 sales volume rose 5% YoY; prices up 1% YoY.
Limestone tax raised raw material costs by ₹47 Cr in Q3, ₹115 Cr in 9MFY26.
news.summary:
The Ramco Cements' Q3FY26 sales volume rose 5% YoY; prices up 1% YoY.
Limestone tax raised raw material costs by ₹47 Cr in Q3, ₹115 Cr in 9MFY26.

news.title:
Ramco Cement's Q3 EBITDA remains steady at 2.8B rupees YOY.
EBITDA margin declined to 13.4% from 14.2% YOY.
news.summary:
Ramco Cement's Q3 EBITDA remains steady at 2.8B rupees YOY.
EBITDA margin declined to 13.4% from 14.2% YOY.

news.title:
The Ramco Cements announced unaudited standalone and consolidated results for Q3 and nine months ended 31 Dec 2025.
Auditors provided an unmodified opinion, and key metrics like total income and net profit were disclosed.
news.summary:
The Ramco Cements announced unaudited standalone and consolidated results for Q3 and nine months ended 31 Dec 2025.
Auditors provided an unmodified opinion, and key metrics like total income and net profit were disclosed.

news.title:
Union Budget 2026-27 raises FY26 capex by 9% to ₹12.2T, boosting cement demand.
Effective capex, including grants, grows 11% to ₹17.1T, aiding cement sector growth.
news.summary:
Union Budget 2026-27 raises FY26 capex by 9% to ₹12.2T, boosting cement demand.
Effective capex, including grants, grows 11% to ₹17.1T, aiding cement sector growth.

news.title:
The Ramco Cements faces CENVAT credit dispute of Rs 2.33 cr; CESTAT remands case.
Similar disputes with authorities total Rs 4.71 cr; latest order received on Jan 20, 2026.
news.summary:
The Ramco Cements faces CENVAT credit dispute of Rs 2.33 cr; CESTAT remands case.
Similar disputes with authorities total Rs 4.71 cr; latest order received on Jan 20, 2026.

news.title:
The Ramco Cements gains approval to include quartzite in limestone mining lease in Andhra Pradesh.
Quartzite will be used for manufactured sand and cement additives; reserve quantity is undetermined.
news.summary:
The Ramco Cements gains approval to include quartzite in limestone mining lease in Andhra Pradesh.
Quartzite will be used for manufactured sand and cement additives; reserve quantity is undetermined.

news.title:
The Ramco Cements sold non-core assets worth ₹514.9 crore to Prestige Estates, surpassing its ₹1,000 crore target with total disposals at ₹1,016.9 crore.
The company plans to sell an additional ₹200 crore worth of assets by February 2026.
news.summary:
The Ramco Cements sold non-core assets worth ₹514.9 crore to Prestige Estates, surpassing its ₹1,000 crore target with total disposals at ₹1,016.9 crore.
The company plans to sell an additional ₹200 crore worth of assets by February 2026.

news.title:
ICRA reaffirmed The Ramco Cements' ratings: AA (Stable) for long-term instruments and A1 for short-term instruments.
Rated instruments include Rs. 2,902 crore term loans, Rs. 3,112 crore short-term, and Rs. 707 crore unallocated limits.
news.summary:
ICRA reaffirmed The Ramco Cements' ratings: AA (Stable) for long-term instruments and A1 for short-term instruments.
Rated instruments include Rs. 2,902 crore term loans, Rs. 3,112 crore short-term, and Rs. 707 crore unallocated limits.

news.title:
Income Tax Dept filed appeals against ITAT order for AY 2011-12; dispute involves Rs 7.35 cr on capital expenditure.
Disputed tax Rs 3.76 cr on industrial promotion assistance; similar disputes of Rs 8.1-21.1 cr pending with Madras HC.
news.summary:
Income Tax Dept filed appeals against ITAT order for AY 2011-12; dispute involves Rs 7.35 cr on capital expenditure.
Disputed tax Rs 3.76 cr on industrial promotion assistance; similar disputes of Rs 8.1-21.1 cr pending with Madras HC.
